@@ -786,6 +786,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2xi6","title":"[EPIC] Widget Trait API Migration: Buffer → Frame","description":"## Background

The Widget trait in ftui-widgets was changed from:
```rust
fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer);
```
to:
```rust
fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame);
```

This change was DIRECTIONALLY CORRECT but POORLY EXECUTED - the trait signature was changed without updating the ~50 widget implementations, leaving the codebase in a broken state.

## Why Frame is Better Than Buffer

Frame provides capabilities that modern TUI widgets need:

1. **buffer: Buffer** - The cell grid (same as before)
2. **pool: &'a mut GraphemePool** - String interning for efficiency
3. **hit_grid: Option<HitGrid>** - Mouse click region registration
4. **cursor_position: Option<(u16, u16)>** - Cursor tracking for inputs
5. **cursor_visible: bool** - Cursor visibility control
6. **degradation: DegradationLevel** - Performance budget adaptation

Without Frame access, widgets CANNOT:
- Register clickable regions for mouse interaction
- Set cursor position (critical for text inputs)
- Adapt rendering based on performance budget
- Use efficient string interning

## Design Decision: Frame is Correct

This is NOT a backwards compatibility issue (we don't care about that per AGENTS.md). 
Frame IS the right abstraction because:

1. **Industry precedent**: Ratatui (dominant Rust TUI framework) uses Frame in Widget trait
2. **Feature requirements**: Hit testing, cursor, degradation are all needed
3. **Future extensibility**: Frame can grow without changing Widget trait again

## The Problem

~50 widget implementations still use `buf: &mut Buffer` instead of `frame: &mut Frame`.
This is a mechanical migration that needs to be done ATOMICALLY and CORRECTLY.

## Success Criteria

- All widgets compile with the new Frame-based signature
- All widgets that need Frame features (hit testing, cursor, degradation) use them
- All widgets that just need buffer access use `frame.buffer`
- All existing tests pass
- No regressions in functionality","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:21:33.037383359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:55:22.934940301Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:55:22.934919502Z","close_reason":"All widgets migrated to Frame-based API. Verified: 38 Frame implementations, 0 Buffer implementations. Workspace compiles clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-10y0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-174b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-1abx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-1rqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ixu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pip","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ryo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ua8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-3coz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-f38d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","depends_on_id":"bd-z8q6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":393,"issue_id":"bd-2xi6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Widget API Migration Status - 2026-02-01

### ✅ MIGRATION COMPLETE

All Widget trait implementations now use Frame-based API:
- `fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame)`

### Completed Tasks (all closed):
- [x] bd-3coz: Audit all Widget implementations
- [x] bd-2ryo: Update shared helper functions
- [x] bd-z8q6: Migrate Category A (simple buffer-only)
- [x] bd-1rqf: Migrate Category B (hit testing)
- [x] bd-10y0: Migrate Category C (cursor control)
- [x] bd-2pip: Add degradation support (Category D)
- [x] bd-f38d: Migrate CachedWidget
- [x] bd-2ua8: Migrate ErrorBoundary
- [x] bd-1abx: Update widget tests
- [x] bd-2ixu: Final verification

### Remaining (non-blocking):
- [ ] bd-174b: Documentation (P1, can be done incrementally)

### Verification Results:
- cargo check --workspace: PASSES
- cargo test --workspace: PASSES
- cargo clippy --workspace: PASSES
- All feature combinations compile

### No Regressions:
- All existing widget functionality preserved
- Hit testing support available via frame.hit_grid
- Cursor control available via frame.cursor_position
- Degradation support available via frame.degradation

**The Widget API migration is functionally complete. Documentation is the only remaining task.**
","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:36:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2yd","title":"Implement StyleSheet registry for named styles","description":"PHASE 4 - STYLESHEET REGISTRY

## Background
StyleSheet provides named style registration similar to CSS classes. Enables theme-able applications and consistent style reuse without hardcoding colors.

## Technical Requirements
- StyleSheet struct with HashMap<StyleId, Style>
- StyleId: either interned string or u32 index
- Register named styles: stylesheet.define('error', Style::new().fg(RED).bold())
- Lookup by name: stylesheet.get('error')
- Style composition: stylesheet.compose(['base', 'error']) merges in order
- Default styles for common semantic names (error, warning, info, success, muted)

## Design Rationale
Named styles decouple visual appearance from widget logic. Themes can override the stylesheet without changing widget code. Matches patterns from Textual/Rich.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StyleSheet with define/get/compose API
- [ ] StyleId type (string or integer based on perf analysis)
- [ ] Thread-safe for read access (RwLock or immutable after init)
- [ ] Default semantic style set
- [ ] Style merging for composition
- [ ] Clear error handling for missing styles

## Integration Points
- Widgets reference styles by name
- Theme loading populates stylesheet
- Buffer.set_style() accepts Style or StyleId

 {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING

## Background
Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics.

## Technical Requirements

### Tracing Infrastructure
- Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging
- Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
- Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline
- Subscriber configuration for:
  - Development: pretty-printed to stderr
  - Testing: captured for assertions
  - Production: JSON structured output (optional)

### Key Instrumentation Points
- Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete
- Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time
- Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level)
- Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events
- State changes: model updates, command execution
- Error conditions: with full context

### Log Format
Development:
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10
  
Testing (structured capture):
  LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... }

### Feature Gates
- feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation
- feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production
- Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead

## Design Rationale
Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines. Feature-gated to avoid overhead in release builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tracing subscriber configuration
- [ ] Frame-level span instrumentation
- [ ] Widget render spans with timing
- [ ] Input parsing tracing
- [ ] Error context capture
- [ ] Test log capture mechanism
- [ ] Feature gate documentation
- [ ] Example showing log output

## Performance Budget
- Disabled: zero overhead (compiled out)
- Enabled: < 1µs per span enter/exit

 {"id":"bd-3emq","title":"Async Validation: Deterministic Concurrency Contract + Golden Trace","description":"## Goal
Make async validation behavior deterministic under a seeded scheduler and prove no stale results can override newer input.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization + Correctness)
- Monotonic validation token per input change; results must include token.
- Apply result only if token matches current input token.
- Record validation events in a trace (start/cancel/complete/apply).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stale results are always ignored with explicit log entries.
- Deterministic trace under fixed seed and scripted input.
- Golden trace checksum matches after refactors (isomorphism proof).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_token_monotonic: tokens strictly increase per input.
- unit_stale_result_ignored: older token never applies.
- unit_trace_contains_all_events: no missing transitions.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Seeded async validator test harness with golden trace hash.
- Log: token, state, duration, decision per validation.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-3exa7","title":"Add VT conformance fixtures: erase-with-bg-color + wide-char edit interactions","description":"Add conformance fixtures for two under-tested areas in frankenterm-core:

1. Erase operations with non-default background color:
   - ED 0/1/2 with cursor bg set to a named/indexed/rgb color
   - EL 0/1/2 with cursor bg set
   - ECH with cursor bg set
   - Verify erased cells have the correct background color

2. Wide character + erase/insert/delete interactions:
   - ECH in the middle of a wide char (should clean up the wide char pair)
   - ICH at a position splitting a wide char
   - DCH removing half of a wide char
   - EL right starting in the middle of a wide char

These are common sources of bugs in terminal emulators and directly support the frankenterm-core correctness goals.

Files touched: tests/fixtures/vt-conformance/erase/ and tests/fixtures/vt-conformance/wide_chars/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:26:21.516292416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:30:58.757277409Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:30:58.757258443Z","close_reason":"Added 8 VT conformance fixtures testing erase operations with non-default background colors (EL 0/1/2 + ED 0/1/2 + ECH with named + RGB backgrounds).
